reason: Response A exhibits hallmarks of Claude Opus's writing style: highly abstract, structurally philosophical prose with references to classical concepts (aporia, ancient philosophers), unusual metaphors ("gravitational disturbance," "proprioceptive"), and a tendency to build toward a sweeping conceptual reframe at the end. Response B is polished and well-written but stays more concrete and phenomenological, reading more like a careful imitation that hits expected beats (the child turning an object, time disappearing) without the same degree of intellectual ambition or conceptual density that Opus typically displays.
